metadata.title: CSS Filter-Based Preview (Exposure, Contrast, Saturation, Hue)

story.iWant: a near-instant preview of how a preset will affect my images using CSS filters
story.soThat: I can quickly assess conversion results without waiting for full processing
story.tasks: 1. Create CSS Filter Mapping Function (AC-1, AC-2) 2. Apply CSS Filter to Preview Image (AC-3) 3. Add Accuracy Disclaimer (AC-4) 4. Browser Compatibility Detection (AC-5) 5. Performance Testing (AC-6) 6. Write Unit Tests (AC-7) 7. Integration with Preset Upload Flow (AC-3) 8. Documentation (AC-4)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-1: Recipe Parameter to CSS Filter Mapping - Exposure → brightness() CSS filter (range: -2.0 to +2.0 → 0% to 200%) - Contrast → contrast() CSS filter (range: -1.0 to +1.0 → 0% to 200%) - Saturation → saturate() CSS filter (range: -1.0 to +1.0 → 0% to 200%) - Hue → hue-rotate() CSS filter (range: -180 to +180 degrees) - Temperature/Tint → sepia() + hue-rotate() approximation - Multiple filters combined in single CSS filter property
AC-2: CSS Filter Function Implementation - JavaScript function recipeToCSS Filters(recipe) converts Recipe object to CSS filter string - Edge case handling (all zeros, out-of-range, invalid parameters)
AC-3: Real-Time Preview Rendering - Preview updates instantly (<100ms) when preset selected - CSS filter applied to preview image element - Smooth transitions between presets (CSS transition 0.3s) - Works across all modern browsers (Chrome 18+, Firefox 35+, Safari 9.1+, Edge 12+)
AC-4: Clear Accuracy Disclaimer - Label prominently displayed: "Approximate preview using CSS filters" - Tooltip/help text explains limitations - No misleading claims about preview accuracy - Disclaimer icon (ℹ️ or ?) next to label
AC-5: Browser Compatibility Testing - CSS filter support verified on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge (latest 2 versions) - Fallback for unsupported browsers (detect with CSS.supports()) - Mobile browser testing (iOS Safari, Chrome for Android, Samsung Internet)
AC-6: Performance Optimization - CSS filter application completes in <100ms - GPU acceleration enabled (will-change: filter) - No JavaScript blocking during filter application - Performance tested on mid-range devices (iPhone 8, Android Snapdragon 665)
AC-7: Unit Test Coverage - recipeToCSS Filters() function fully tested - Edge case tests (null recipe, invalid parameters, extreme values) - Coverage target: 100% for recipeToCSS Filters()

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Image Preview System (Phase 1)

docs.doc: Epic 11 adds visual preset preview using CSS filters for instant, approximate previews—trading pixel-perfect accuracy for speed and simplicity. Implementation leverages browser-native CSS filter functions (brightness, contrast, saturate, hue-rotate, sepia) to approximate UniversalRecipe parameters.

CSS Filter Mapping (preview.js): Function recipeToCSS Filters(recipe) maps UniversalRecipe parameters to CSS filter string. Exposure (-2.0 to +2.0) → brightness(0% to 200%), Contrast (-1.0 to +1.0) → contrast(0% to 200%), Saturation (-1.0 to +1.0) → saturate(0% to 200%), Temperature/tint → sepia() + hue-rotate() approximation.
New modules: web/js/preview.js (Preview modal, CSS filter mapping, slider logic), web/css/preview.css (Modal overlay, slider styles, tab navigation), web/images/preview-*.webp (Reference images)

Performance targets: Preview rendering <100ms (instant feel), CSS filter application <10ms (browser-native, GPU-accelerated), Slider interaction 60fps (16ms per frame), Reference image load <500ms on 3G

Frontend framework: Vanilla JavaScript (zero dependencies). Browser compatibility target: Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge (latest 2 versions). WASM loading strategy: Eager loading + Service Worker caching. Required browser features: WebAssembly, File API, drag-and-drop events, Promises/async-await

CSS filter support: Chrome 18+ (filter support since 2012), Firefox 35+ (filter support since 2015), Safari 9.1+ (filter support since 2016), Edge 12+ (filter support since 2015). Web API support verified for WebAssembly MVP, FileReader API, Blob API, Drag and Drop Events, CSS Custom Properties.

Preview modal (Story 11.3) must be keyboard accessible (AC-7 from Story 10.7). Preview slider (Story 11.4) must support arrow keys (AC-1 from Story 10.7). Preview disclaimer (Story 11.1) must use semantic HTML (AC-4 from Story 10.7). CSS transitions (Story 11.1) must respect prefers-reduced-motion (AC-7 from Story 10.7).

artifact.reason: Main web interface entry point - shows how to integrate new preview.js module with existing upload workflow and event system. Uses ES6 module imports, custom events (fileLoaded, formatDetected), and WASM integration patterns that preview.js should follow.
Format detection using WASM - demonstrates how to call WASM functions from JavaScript and handle results. Preview.js will need to parse UniversalRecipe from uploaded files using similar WASM calls.
Parameter display module from Story 2-4 - shows how to extract and display UniversalRecipe parameters. Preview.js will reuse similar parameter extraction logic to map recipe values to CSS filters.

Browser compatibility detection from Story 6-4 - demonstrates how to check for browser features using feature detection. Preview.js should use similar pattern for CSS.supports() check for filter support.

Main stylesheet for web interface - defines existing UI patterns (color scheme, typography, layout) that preview.css should follow for consistency. Includes CSS custom properties, Flexbox patterns, and responsive breakpoints.

Unsupported browser screen styling from Story 6-4 - shows graceful degradation pattern for browsers without required features. Preview.js should use similar pattern for browsers without CSS filter support.
WASM conversion orchestration - demonstrates async/await patterns for WASM function calls, error handling, and progress feedback. Preview.js will call WASM parser functions using similar patterns.
Mobile responsive adaptations from Story 2-10 - shows touch detection and orientation handling. Preview slider (Story 11.4) will need similar touch support for mobile devices.

package.name: CSS Filter Effects Module Level 1
package.usage: Browser-native CSS filters (brightness, contrast, saturate, hue-rotate, sepia)

package.name: File API
package.usage: FileReader, Blob, URL.createObjectURL for file handling

dependencies.ecosystem: <package name="Lighthouse CI" version="latest" usage="Performance auditing for <100ms preview rendering target" />

story-context.constraints: - Must maintain <100ms preview rendering (instant feel per AC-3, AC-6) - CSS filters only - no JavaScript image processing in Phase 1 (per tech spec) - Works across all modern browsers: Chrome 18+, Firefox 35+, Safari 9.1+, Edge 12+ (AC-3, AC-5) - GPU acceleration required via will-change: filter (AC-6) - No external image requests - reference images bundled in web/images/ (per tech spec) - Follow Epic 2 patterns: ES6 modules, vanilla JavaScript, modular architecture (per tech spec Decision 1) - Clear accuracy disclaimer required - no misleading claims (AC-4) - Temperature/tint approximation only - CSS lacks true color temperature adjustment (per dev notes) - Tone curves not supported in Phase 1 - CSS filters don't support arbitrary curves (per tech spec Out of Scope) - Must respect prefers-reduced-motion for CSS transitions (per Story 10.7 integration) - Keyboard accessible - Esc to close modal, Tab navigation (per Story 10.7) - Mobile-friendly - touch support for slider (defer to Story 11.4)

interfaces.interface: Convert UniversalRecipe parameters to CSS filter string. Returns combined filter string (e.g., "brightness(150%) contrast(130%)") or "none" if no adjustments.
Apply CSS filter to preview image element. Sets previewImage.style.filter to result of recipeToCSS Filters().

interface.name: clamp(value, min, max)
interface.signature: clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number): number

interfaces.interface: Clamp numeric value to min/max range. Used to ensure CSS filter values stay within valid range (0-200%).
Detect CSS filter support using CSS.supports() API. Returns true if browser supports CSS filters, false otherwise.
Existing browser compatibility detection from Story 6-4. Checks WebAssembly, FileReader, and Blob support. Preview.js should extend this pattern for CSS filter detection.

interface.name: UniversalRecipe
interface.signature: { exposure: number, contrast: number, saturation: number, hue: number, temperature: number, tint: number, ... }

interfaces.interface: Core data structure from Epic 1. Contains all recipe parameters that preview.js will map to CSS filters. JavaScript receives as JSON from WASM parser.

tests.standards: Unit testing with Jest for JavaScript functions. Focus on recipeToCSS Filters() function with 100% coverage target (AC-7). Test edge cases: null recipe, zero parameters, out-of-range values, invalid parameter types. Integration testing via manual browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge). Performance testing with Lighthouse CI (<100ms target) and manual device testing (iPhone 8, Android mid-range). Browser compatibility testing using CSS.supports() feature detection and manual cross-browser verification.
tests.locations: web/js/__tests__/preview.test.js - Unit tests for preview.js functions web/static/*.test.js - Existing test pattern from Epic 2 (currently no tests, establish pattern)

ideas.test: Unit test: Exposure +0.5 maps to "brightness(150%)"
Unit test: Contrast +0.3 maps to "contrast(130%)"
Unit test: Saturation -0.5 maps to "saturate(50%)"
Unit test: Hue +30 maps to "hue-rotate(30deg)"
Unit test: Temperature +20 maps to "sepia(6) hue-rotate(10deg)"
Unit test: Multiple parameters combine correctly
Unit test: Zero parameters return "none"
Unit test: Out-of-range exposure (+5.0) clamps to "brightness(200%)"
Unit test: Negative exposure (-3.0) clamps to "brightness(0%)"
Unit test: Null recipe returns "none"
Unit test: Invalid parameter types skip gracefully
Manual test: Preview updates in <100ms when preset selected
Manual test: CSS filter applied to preview image element
Manual test: Smooth transitions between presets (CSS transition)
Manual test: Disclaimer label visible in modal
Manual test: Tooltip explains CSS filter limitations
Manual test: CSS filter support verified on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
Manual test: Fallback message for unsupported browsers (CSS.supports())
Manual test: Mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Chrome Android, Samsung Internet)
Performance test: Filter application <100ms (performance.now())
Performance test: GPU acceleration enabled (Chrome DevTools)
Performance test: Mid-range devices (iPhone 8, Android Snapdragon 665)
Unit test: 100% coverage for recipeToCSS Filters() function
